source.definition: to formally end a contract or employment

explanation.sourceText: The English word "resign" comes from the Latin verb "resignare," meaning "to unseal, cancel, or give up." It entered English through Old French and has been used since the late Middle Ages to denote formally giving up a position or agreement.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „resign” provine din verbul latin „resignare”, care înseamnă „a desigila, anula sau renunța”. A intrat în limba engleză prin franceza veche și este folosit încă din Evul Mediu târziu pentru a desemna renunțarea formală la o poziție sau un acord.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used in formal contexts related to ending employment contracts or subscriptions.

target.definition: a încheia un contract sau o angajare

explanation.sourceText: Derived from the Latin "renuntiare," meaning "to announce or give up," it is used in formal contexts.
explanation.targetText: Vom lateinischen „renuntiare“ abstammend, bedeutet es „abmelden“ oder „aufkündigen“ und wird in formalen Kontexten verwendet.

target.additional: Verb regulat. Este folosit frecvent pentru a anula contracte, abonamente sau alte acorduri.

explanation.sourceText: Used to indicate the ending of a job or position.

examples.targetText: El renunță la locul său de muncă.

explanation.sourceText: Used to indicate the termination of a contract.

examples.targetText: El renunța la locul său de muncă.

learningTips.text: Remember "a renunța" as the formal way to say "resign" in Romanian — it sounds like "renounce," which means to give up or quit in English.
Practice sentences like "Renunț la abonament" to get comfortable with ending contracts or subscriptions.
Think of "renunța" as similar to "renounce" plus "announce" — you formally announce giving something up.
Use the phrase "El renunță la locul său de muncă" to remember it means "He resigns from his job."
